The first person to answer 20 quiz questions correctly will win

formats.descriptions:
SECTION 3   Questions 21-30

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Course Feedback

questions.content: One reason why Spiros felt happy about his marketing presentation was that

options.content: the presentation was the best in his group.

questions.content: What surprised Hiroko about the other students’ presentation?

options.content: They didn't look at the audience enough.

questions.content: After she gave her presentation, she felt
How does Spiros felt his performance in tutorials?
Why can the other students participate so easily in discussions?

options.content: They are polite to each other.
They agree to take turns in speaking.

questions.content: Why is Hiroko feeling more positive about tutorials now?

options.content: She finds the other students' opinions more interesting.
She is making more of a contribution.
The tutor includes her in the discussion.

questions.content: To help her understand lectures, Hiroko

options.content: had extra tutorials with her lectures.
borrowed lecture notes from other students.

questions.content: What does Spiros think of his reading skills?

options.content: He reads faster than he used to.
It still takes him a long time to read.
He tends to struggle with new vocabulary.

questions.content: Hiroko thinks that in the reading classes the students should

options.content: read more in their subject areas.

formats.descriptions:
SECTION 4   Questions 31-40

Complete the notes below.

Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
formats.content:
Mass Strandings of Whales and Dolphins

Mass strandings: situations where groups of whales, dolphins, etc. Swim into the beach and die

Common in areas where the ...... can change quickly

Several other theories:

 

Parasites

e.g. some parasites can affect marine animals’ ...... , which they depend on for navigation.

 

Toxins

Poisons from ...... or ...... are commonly consumed by whales

e.g. Cape Cod(1988)-whales were killed by saxitoxin.

 

Accidental Strandings

Animals may follow prey ashore, e.g. Thurston(1995)

Unlikely because the majority of animals were not ...... when they stranded.

 

Human Activity

...... from military tests are linked to some recent strandings

The Bahamas(2000) stranding was unusual because the whales

·were all ......  

·were not in a ......   

 

Group Behaviour

·More strandings in the most ...... species of whales

·1994 dolphin stranding -- only the ...... was ill.

 

Further Reading

Marine Mammals Ashore(Connor)--gives information about stranding ......
